name: New York
tagline: The World's Cultural Capital — Endless Options at Every Price Point
description: New York City is the ultimate test of a FIRE portfolio — brutally expensive but offering an unmatched density of culture, cuisine, and connection. With world-class healthcare at Mount Sinai and NYU Langone, no car needed thanks to the subway, and a social scene that never sleeps, NYC rewards those with the budget to match its ambitions. The high cost of living means your withdrawal rate works harder, but no other city on earth packs this much life per square block.

quickFacts.visa: No visa required for US citizens
quickFacts.internetSpeed: 300-1000 Mbps fiber available via Verizon Fios and Spectrum
quickFacts.safetyRating: Moderately Safe — vastly safer than its reputation; safest large city in America by per-capita crime rates
quickFacts.nearestAirport: John F. Kennedy International (JFK) — 45 min from Manhattan; LaGuardia (LGA) — 30 min; Newark (EWR) — 35 min
quickFacts.climate: Humid continental with cold winters (-3 to 4°C), hot humid summers (24-30°C), and beautiful spring/fall seasons. Four distinct seasons.

housing.description: Compact studio or room in a shared apartment in Washington Heights, Astoria, or Bushwick — character-rich neighborhoods with long commutes

dining.description: Dollar-slice pizza, Chinatown dumpling houses, halal carts, and bodega breakfast sandwiches — NYC's best cheap eats are legendary

healthcare.description: ACA marketplace bronze plan through NY State of Health, community health center for routine care

transportation.description: Unlimited MetroCard for subway and bus — this is your lifeline across all five boroughs

entertainment.description: Free museum nights (Met is pay-what-you-wish), Central Park, free concerts in Prospect Park, $30 rush tickets on Broadway

1M.lifestyle: A tight budget in one of the most expensive cities in the country. You share an apartment or rent a small studio in an outer neighborhood, cook most meals at home, and rely on the subway to get everywhere. The upside is real: free museums, cheap dumplings in Flushing, and Central Park cost nothing. But $3,333 a month in NYC means watching every dollar, and state plus city income taxes shrink your effective budget further.

housing.description: True 1-bedroom apartment in Harlem, Park Slope, Long Island City, or the East Village — a real neighborhood with personality

dining.description: Regular dinners at neighborhood bistros and ramen spots, weekend brunch culture, cocktail bars in the West Village

healthcare.description: ACA silver plan with access to NYU Langone or Mount Sinai network, dental included, manageable deductibles

transportation.description: Unlimited MetroCard plus occasional Lyft rides and Citi Bike membership for crosstown trips

entertainment.description: Broadway shows monthly, MoMA and Whitney memberships, jazz at Village Vanguard, comedy at the Cellar, Mets or Nets games

2M.lifestyle: You can afford your own one-bedroom in a good neighborhood like the East Village or Park Slope, eat out several times a week at solid restaurants, and attend Broadway shows or jazz clubs regularly. You live comfortably without extravagance. A Citi Bike membership and MetroCard keep you mobile, and the cultural density means there is always something to do. New York's combined state and city taxes are a real drag on this budget, but the lifestyle is genuinely enjoyable.

housing.description: Spacious 1-bedroom or small 2-bedroom in the West Village, Upper West Side, or Brooklyn Heights — classic NYC living at its finest

dining.description: Regular tables at Balthazar, Gramercy Tavern, and neighborhood gems. Omakase splurges, wine-paired tasting menus, and supper clubs

healthcare.description: Premium PPO with access to top-tier specialists at Memorial Sloan Kettering and Hospital for Special Surgery, concierge primary care

transportation.description: MetroCard and Citi Bike plus regular Lyft rides, weekend car service to the Hamptons or Hudson Valley

entertainment.description: Orchestra seats on Broadway, premium jazz club tables, Met Opera balcony seats, SoHo gallery openings, seasonal Governors Island events

3M.lifestyle: A comfortable apartment in the West Village, Upper West Side, or Brooklyn Heights puts you in a desirable part of the city. You dine at well-regarded restaurants several nights a week, hold museum memberships, and see orchestra-level Broadway shows regularly. Weekend trips to the Hudson Valley or Long Island are feasible. At $10,000 a month, New York is genuinely affluent living, though still not lavish by Manhattan standards.

housing.description: Luxury doorman 2-bedroom in Tribeca, a brownstone floor-through in the West Village, or a Park Avenue classic six with Central Park proximity

dining.description: Private chef for weeknight dinners, Le Bernardin and Eleven Madison Park for special occasions, membership at exclusive dining clubs

healthcare.description: Concierge medicine through Sollis Health or One Medical, full dental and vision, annual executive physical at NYU Langone

transportation.description: Black car service on demand via Blade or private driver, Jitney membership to the Hamptons, occasional helicopter transfers

entertainment.description: Patron-level memberships at the Met and MoMA, private box at Madison Square Garden, front-row galas, exclusive art fair previews at Frieze

domesticHelp.description: Full-time housekeeper, weekly deep cleaning, laundry service, dog walker if needed

luxuryExtras.description: Equinox+ membership, personal stylist, premium co-working at NeueHouse, annual wardrobe refresh, weekend house in Hudson Valley

5M.lifestyle: A large apartment in a doorman building in Tribeca or a floor-through in the West Village. You dine at top restaurants regularly, have a housekeeper several times a week, and fly business class for longer trips. Weekends might mean a rental in the Hamptons or Hudson Valley. This is upper-affluent New York -- you live very well and rarely think about restaurant prices, but you are renting, not buying, in the most expensive neighborhoods.

housing.description: Trophy penthouse in Tribeca or a full-floor residence on Central Park West with panoramic park and skyline views, white-glove concierge building

dining.description: Full-time private chef with Michelin pedigree, curated wine cellar, exclusive supper club circuit, flying in truffles and wagyu for dinner parties

healthcare.description: Elite concierge medical team, longevity protocols, fly-anywhere specialist access, private suite at NYP or Mount Sinai if ever needed

transportation.description: Full-time private driver with luxury sedan, Blade helicopter membership, NetJets card for East Coast and European hops

entertainment.description: Board seats at major cultural institutions, private art collection curation, major philanthropic galas, art patronage

domesticHelp.description: Full household staff: estate manager, private chef, housekeeper, personal assistant, and weekend house caretaker

luxuryExtras.description: Hamptons estate maintenance, bespoke tailoring at Savile Row on trips, private art advisor, charitable foundation, interior designer on retainer

10M.lifestyle: A premium apartment on Central Park West or a large Tribeca residence in a white-glove building. You have a housekeeper, eat at the city's best restaurants without hesitation, and travel business or first class. A cook comes in several times a week. You support cultural institutions as a donor and attend galas comfortably. This is the life of a successful professional who never worries about money in New York -- wealthy and comfortable, not ostentatious.
